```stances
{"id":"i1","stance":"support","rationale":"Neither original code nor Lua ever issues SREM; both only SADD, so members accumulate until the whole-set TTL expires, capping distinct users per window rather than tracking concurrency, contradicting the 'ConcurrentUserLimiter' name; the review never flags this."}
{"id":"i3","stance":"support","rationale":"expire(SET_KEY,ttl) is issued before set(TTL_KEY,ttl), so TTL_KEY's absolute expiry is marginally later; if SET_KEY expires first, a new SADD recreates it while hasKey(TTL_KEY) is still true, skipping expire and leaving the recreated set without TTL until the marker expires."}
{"id":"i4","stance":"support_with_revision","rationale":"The Lua's SISMEMBER-then-return-1 does admit existing members before any size check, a genuine and undocumented divergence from the original's reject-all-when-over-capacity, but it is the intended fix for the lockout bug, not itself a defect.","revision":{"severity":"low","claim":"Lua admits existing members before the size check, an undocumented (though arguably desirable) behavior change from the original's reject-all-over-capacity that the review does not call out."}}
{"id":"i5","stance":"support","rationale":"The five ops (SADD+hasKey+expire+set+SCARD) occur only for the first-ever user; a returning user does 2 (SADD+SCARD) and a new user with the marker present does 3, so 'five to one' is worst case, not steady state."}
{"id":"i6","stance":"support","rationale":"A crash after SADD before expire also leaves TTL_KEY unwritten, so the next distinct user finds ttlMarkerExists=false and re-issues expire(SET_KEY,ttl), repairing the leak unless all subsequent traffic is from already-present members."}
```
